Diphylleia myrtifolia, also known as Diphylleia cymosa, is a species of flowering plant in the barberry family (Berberidaceae), native to the Himalayas and eastern Asia. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Diphylleia myrtifolia include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Diphylleia myrtifolia is used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
